trigger_keywords: técnica puede utilizarse para reducir la intensidad tánica en vinos tintos

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A red wine receives small, controlled doses of oxygen during maturation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Controlled oxygen supports reactions between tannins, pigments, and other phenolic compounds, encouraging polymerisation and integration.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Tannins can feel less aggressive and more integrated even though the wine retains phenolic structure.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Controlled microoxygenation can soften perceived tannin intensity by promoting phenolic polymerisation and integration.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
